prompt:
Need your help on a piece of test infrastructure. Our services talk through Kafka in prod, and every integration test spins up the whole thing in Docker — 40 seconds before the first assertion runs. I want an in-memory stand-in with the same consumption model so the tests exercise real offset logic. Module go-eventbus, package eventbus, stdlib only.

Model: named topics, each an append-only event log with dense per-topic offsets starting at 0. Consumers are named groups with a cursor per (topic, group); delivery is pull-based and at-least-once — events you haven't acked get redelivered on the next poll.

Surface:
- NewBus() *Bus
- Publish(topic string, payload []byte) (int64, error) — returns the assigned offset; empty topic is an error; the bus stores a copy of the payload (publishers reuse buffers, that bit us before).
- Poll(topic, group string, max int) ([]Event, error) with Event{Offset int64; Payload []byte} — up to max events from the group's cursor, in order. max < 1 or empty group = error. Topic nobody's published to = empty result, NOT an error (tests poll before the producer starts). Delivered payloads are copies too.
- Ack(topic, group string, through int64) error — commit everything up to AND including offset `through`; next poll starts after it. Acking an offset that was never published (or negative) is an error that moves nothing. Acking at or below what's already committed is a harmless no-op — cursors never move backwards via Ack.
- Seek(topic, group string, offset int64) error — set the group's cursor so the next poll starts at `offset`; this is how tests replay history. Valid range is 0..topic length inclusive (seeking to the end = start from 'now'); outside that, error.
- Position(topic, group string) int64 — where the group's cursor is; a never-seen group is at 0. Len(topic string) int64 and Topics() []string (sorted).

Groups on the same topic are fully independent, the same group name on two topics is independent, and everything must be safe with concurrent publishers and pollers hammering it — concurrent publishes must produce dense, distinct offsets with no gaps. CI runs go test -race -timeout 30s ./... and bus_test.go + concurrency_test.go are the contract, don't touch them. Split the topic/log bookkeeping and the consumer-group cursor logic into separate files so this stays readable when we add TTLs later.
